task_yaml.task:
We need to decide which trade show to invest in next quarter and I've
been staring at three prospectus PDFs without making progress. Use the
the PDF extraction skill to pull the facts out of the PDFs and the
business writing skill to draft a recommendation I can hand to my VP.

The files are under /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/:
acvo_exhibitor_2025.pdf, cda_anaheim_exhibitor_2025.pdf, and
texas_charter_prospectus_2025.pdf, plus booth_roi_assumptions.csv with
our current lead-volume and gross-margin assumptions.

Pull out commercial terms, schedule constraints, package inclusions,
and anything else that affects ROI, then put the three shows side by
side in /tmp_workspace/results/compare.md.

Include a second ROI table covering all three shows with total cash
outlay, expected gross margin, expected net contribution, break-even
number of deals, and break-even number of qualified leads. Use the CSV
assumptions for the model and the PDF booth fees for the cash outlay.
For break-even qualified leads, compute it directly as the ceiling of
cash_outlay divided by per-lead margin, where per-lead margin is gross
margin per deal multiplied by the lead-to-deal conversion rate. Do not
derive it from break-even deals.

Then write a short recommendation paragraph of at most 8 sentences
naming your top pick and runner-up with reasoning, and cite at least
two concrete values from the comparison or ROI tables.

Also save /tmp_workspace/results/roi_model.xlsx with a sheet named ROI.
One row per show, formula-backed columns for booth fee, staff/travel
cost, total cash outlay, expected gross margin, expected net
contribution, break-even deals, and break-even qualified leads. I want
to be able to change the lead or margin assumptions later and have the
model recalculate.

Before you wrap up, add a short narrative section at the bottom of
compare.md, separate from the recommendation paragraph, walking
through how the three shows stack up. For each show cover booth
economics with the fee, deposit and package contents; the traffic
estimate including expected attendance, leads and conversion to
deals; vertical alignment for our pet/veterinary focus; timing and
logistics including schedule, setup, teardown and when cancellations
harden; and the ROI break-even math with deals needed and qualified
leads needed. Tie each point to concrete numbers from the comparison
and ROI tables so the recommendation is auditable.

Once you have the comparison ready, also use the email-drafting skill
to draft a quick email I can forward to the marketing team with the
top-line recommendation.
